meta.title: The F# Working Set
meta.subtitle: a discipline-driven Python→F# library catalogue
meta.note: Type signatures are stylized identifying marks, not compilable definitions. Verify maintenance/currency for fast-moving areas (web frameworks) before asserting 'best' or 'active'.

items.name: FsToolkit.ErrorHandling
items.use: You want Result/Option/asyncResult CEs and traversals as the spine of every boundary.

items.name: FSharpPlus
items.use: You want lawful, generic abstractions (functor/monad/monoid) across your own types.

items.name: FSharpx.Collections
items.use: You need persistent/immutable structures beyond what FSharp.Core ships.

items.name: FSharp.SystemTextJson
items.use: You're on System.Text.Json and want DUs/options/records to just work.

items.name: Thoth.Json
items.use: You want explicit, Elm-style codecs you control rather than reflection.

items.name: FsCodec
items.use: Event-stream encode/decode with contract versioning (CDC/ES paths).

items.name: Validus
items.use: You want composable, accumulating validators returning a typed result.

items.name: FSharp.Data
items.use: Parse-at-the-boundary from CSV/JSON/XML/HTML samples via type providers.

items.name: Refined (roll-your-own)
items.use: You need invariants as proofs at the edge — your own discipline, no package.

items.name: FsHttp
items.use: Any HTTP call — the DSL + CE make requests genuinely pleasant.

items.name: Falco
items.use: Lightweight, functional-first HTTP. v5 ships first-class HTMX + OpenAPI.

items.name: Oxpecker
items.use: Giraffe's successor — same model, optimized core, endpoint routing, fullstack.

items.name: Giraffe
items.use: Battle-tested combinator web on ASP.NET Core (now back under active maintenance).

items.name: Saturn
items.use: You want a Rails/Django-shaped MVC convention layer over Giraffe.

items.name: Deedle
items.use: DataFrame/time-series work with a typed-ish frame and series algebra.

items.name: Math.NET Numerics
items.use: Linear algebra, distributions, FFT, solvers — the numpy/scipy core.

items.name: FSharp.Stats
items.use: Statistics, fitting, ML primitives with an F#-native API.

items.name: Parquet.Net
items.use: Read/write Parquet directly (content-addressed columnar tier).

items.name: DuckDB.NET
items.use: Embedded analytical SQL over Parquet/Arrow without a server.

items.name: Plotly.NET
items.use: Publication-grade interactive charts — the matplotlib/plotly answer.

items.name: ML.NET
items.use: Classical ML pipelines (classification, regression) on .NET data.

items.name: TorchSharp
items.use: Full libtorch bindings when you need real deep-learning tensors.

items.name: DiffSharp / Furnace
items.use: F#-native differentiable programming and autodiff, idiomatic and composable.

items.name: Dapper.FSharp
items.use: Typed query CEs over Dapper without writing raw SQL strings.

items.name: Donald
items.use: Thin, functional ADO.NET wrapper — explicit, no magic.

items.name: SqlHydra
items.use: Generate F# records + typed query DSL straight from your DB schema.

items.name: Npgsql
items.use: The Postgres driver underneath everything (catalog substrate).

items.name: Expecto
items.use: The runner — tests are values, parallel by default, great with FsCheck.

items.name: FsCheck
items.use: Property-based testing — LWW/arity invariants live here.

items.name: Hedgehog
items.use: Property testing with integrated shrinking and explicit generators.

items.name: Unquote
items.use: Assertions via quotations — failures show the reduced expression.

items.name: Argu
items.use: Declarative, typed command-line parsing from a DU spec.

items.name: FsConfig
items.use: Bind environment/app config into typed records with validation.

items.name: NodaTime
items.use: Any time/zone/duration logic — never touch raw DateTime again.

items.name: Serilog
items.use: Structured logging with rich sinks (or stay on a Writer-monad lineage).

items.name: FSharp.Control.TaskSeq
items.use: Lazy async sequences — backfill firehoses and streamed readers.

items.name: Hopac
items.use: High-throughput CSP-style concurrency when Async isn't enough.

items.name: FsKafka
items.use: Idiomatic Confluent.Kafka wrapper for batched consume/produce.

items.name: Propulsion
items.use: Stream scheduler: co-partitioned, checkpointed projection (CDC arm).

items.name: Equinox
items.use: Event-sourced deciders with consistent state over a store.

items.name: Fantomas
items.use: The formatter — the black/ruff of F#.

items.name: Paket
items.use: Deterministic, transitive-pinned dependency management.

items.name: .NET Interactive
items.use: Polyglot notebooks — the Jupyter story for F#.

items.name: Math.NET Symbolics
items.use: Symbolic algebra/CAS when you need sympy-style manipulation.

items.name: FSharp.Data.GraphQL
items.use: Build or consume GraphQL with an F#-typed schema.

items.name: Feliz + Fable
items.use: F# on the frontend — typed React via Fable (or Oxpecker.Solid).
